news.title: Kawasaki Heavy, Nvidia plan Silicon Valley robotics center, Nikkei reports
news.summary: May 21 () - Japan's Kawasaki Heavy Industries will partner with ‌Nvidia to develop solutions integrating ‌robotics with physical artificial intelligence, and will ​set up a joint development center in Silicon Valley, the Nikkei newspaper reported on Thursday. The collaboration will ‌initially focus ⁠on medical and mobility fields, with Nvidia's simulation technology ⁠to be applied to Kawasaki Heavy Industries' Corleo, a four-legged personal ​mobility robot ​under development, ​Nikkei added.

news.title: Bitcoin Depot bankruptcy signals reckoning for crypto ATMs, Roshan Dharia says
news.summary: Investing.com -- The collapse of Bitcoin Depot is intensifying concerns that the traditional crypto ATM business model may no longer be viable in the United States under mounting regulatory scrutiny. Roshan Dharia, chief executive of Echo Base and a seasoned restructuring advisor, told Investing.com the company’s bankruptcy could be an early warning sign for operators struggling with shrinking transaction spreads, escalating compliance costs, and growing exposure to fraud-related enforcement.

news.title: Mortgage Rates Hit a Nine-Month High in Blow to Prime Buying Season
news.summary: The average rate on a 30-year fixed mortgage rose to 6.51% this week, from 6.36% last week, Freddie Mac said Thursday.  Higher mortgage rates and expensive home prices are keeping many would-be buyers on the sidelines.  Rising costs in many places for home insurance and property taxes have also spooked buyers.
